rationale: The candidate employs the same core machinery as the query's method re-expressed in the hypothetical: using probability generating functions and branching-process theory to analyze phase transitions (invasion threshold), component sizes (outbreak sizes), and reachability (transmission generations) in networks with arbitrary degree/offspring distributions. Its object—the transmission structure of infectious diseases in host populations—is genuinely different from the query's random graphs of social/internet networks, representing a cross-field transfer.
